_comment: Plugin manifest (authored by the plugin author). 'version' is the plugin semver (release-only); install-plugin reads 'setup'.
name: visual-qa
description: Visual end-to-end testing 'eyes': a pluggable vision-language model (VLM) behind an OpenAI-compatible endpoint on any NVIDIA GPU system, plus look/assert_visual tools, a GPU-aware model recommender (pick by VRAM + architecture + task, user chooses from a shortlist), and a capture->assert verify workflow for web, native/TUI, and mobile UIs. UI-strong VLMs (Qwen3-VL / Holo1.5) for stills/OCR; NVIDIA Cosmos (Reason2 / Cosmos 3) for temporal/physical video.

notes: Works on any NVIDIA GPU system (DGX Spark, workstation RTX, datacenter A100/H100, or a cloud endpoint). Needs a reachable OpenAI-compatible VLM endpoint - see README.md.

Choose the model per system: run `eyes.py recommend --vram <GB> --arch <arch> --task <task>` (or `--detect`), present the shortlist, and let the user pick. Don't hard-code a model. Catalog is editable in shared/models.json.
Quant follows the GPU arch: Blackwell -> NVFP4, Hopper/Ada -> FP8, Ampere/older -> AWQ int4.
The eyes client (shared/eyes.py) runs via `uv run` and pulls the `mcp` SDK on first run (PEP 723 inline deps); it talks plain HTTP+JSON, no vendor SDK.
On memory-bandwidth-bound boxes (e.g. DGX Spark, ~273 GB/s) keep concurrent vision streams <= 2 at high vision-token budgets.
Web capture reuses the Playwright MCP (already in the framework MCP template). Native/TUI capture uses screen-capture/ffmpeg; mobile uses simulator/device screen recording.

resources.prompt: OpenAI-compatible VLM endpoint base URL (the GPU box serving the model)
default VLM model id for stills/UI (pick via `eyes.py recommend`)
model id for temporal/flow video (e.g. nvidia/Cosmos3-Nano reasoner or nvidia/Cosmos-Reason2-8B)
